capability: the novelty reserve tracks the recent RATE of newcomers (γ-decayed count of first-appearances) instead of the hand-rolled constant NOVELTY_RESERVE
claim: a rate-derived reserve improves predictive surprise where novelty is positively autocorrelated (bursts) and regresses it where novelty is anti-correlated (maximally-spaced newcomers), because it adapts to the RATE of newcomers but is blind to their AUTOCORRELATION
dissociation: sign(deltaSurprisal[stream-01]) != sign(deltaSurprisal[stream-02]) — the SAME mechanism helps one regime and hurts the other; the net sign over any corpus is set by that corpus's novelty autocorrelation, which a blanket reserve cannot know
control: opts.signalReserve OFF must reproduce the text goldens byte-for-byte (the parity gate); the deltas above are measured strictly against that off baseline, so a non-zero delta is the on-path's own effect and nothing else
mechanism: reserve = noveltyAmplitude(firstSeen, at, gamma) = Sum_{f<at} gamma^(at-1-f); fed as `novelty` into surpriseAt/forwardDist (proposition field) and into the figure-field Z/pNovel; cold-start falls back to the SEED NOVELTY_RESERVE
disposition: NOT PROMOTED — (1) ON breaks byte-identical parity on the 202 text goldens (the cardinal gate), and (2) a blanket default would regress anti-correlated corpora; ships OPT-IN behind opts.signalReserve and is kept as the recorded variant the next cycle (an autocorrelation-aware reserve) improves on
